title: Lex Specialis — node metadata for conflict resolution
description: Nodes that can participate in legal conflict (LawInstrument, Section, TextChunk in pilot ETL) carry authority_class, authority_weight_numeric, specificity_rank, and lex-effective dates. Guides and drafts are weaker than statute; amendments/consolidated editions are modelled with higher specificity_rank. Phase 4+ can combine these with OVERRIDES/MODIFIES edges.

node_properties.authority_class: Closed vocabulary string inferred from tier, instrument_type, doc_type, is_draft (see scripts/kg_lex_specialis_lib.py).
node_properties.authority_weight_numeric: Float in [0, 1]. Uses manifest authority_weight when parseable; else default for authority_class.
node_properties.specificity_rank: Integer; higher means more specific for tie-breaking within the same time window.
node_properties.lex_effective_from: ISO-ish date string from effective_start_date / effective_from when present; null if unknown.
node_properties.lex_effective_to: From effective_end_date when non-empty; else null.

notes: When two rules overlap in time, prefer higher specificity_rank; if tied, prefer later entry in precedence_order.
OVERRIDES edges (Step 3c) can encode explicit 'beats' relationships; these fields support ranking when traversing without explicit override edges.
